title: ilex nothophoeboides
intro: Ilex nothophoeboides, also known as the Australian Holly, is a plant species native to the eastern regions of Australia. It is a member of the holly family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's leaves and berries have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to their high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. Ilex nothophoeboides is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Ilex nothophoeboides, which appears to be a synonym for Ilex rotunda, has significant development value on lipid-lowering, hepatoprotection, and anti-inflammation.
